reviews.review_description: This game is fantastic. My only major issue was having issues grabbing items and even having to get into the floor even with it set on the lowest possible height setting. A grab reticle that allowed the item you were looking at to be retrieved would have been a huge win for the gameplay here.

The game itself and the puzzles felt a bit repetitive to me. Would have loved to see some different puzzles and it took me a little time to figure out the bubbles and whale flying part. 

All in all loved some parts of the game and didn’t like others but still a fun game to play and finish to the end. 

Do think it’s more a game for 10-13yo kids than for adults though?

reviews.review_title: Good Puzzle Game with some bugs
reviews.review_description:
The gravity mechanic is pretty cool, 
make sure to take breaks if you get dizzy!
Great use of the 3D VR space in the puzzle design.

EDITING UPDATED AFTER FIRST review starting out caus I did go reinstall and see if things were different for me as for the boss fight and even though a lot think it’s easy it’s not for me and that matters or should. Improved the system w bubbles but still same issue I’m confused all together on what am I supposed to hit other then the purple guys and how do u hav time to wait one second  to charge bubbles while having to teleport also to not fall off and go thru loading scene again and again😫 I guess not enough had issues w same thing to get a skip the fight so can finish this story and puzzles caus that’s the things I absolutely adored bout this game. Gav stars bk but bummed dnt hav option to get past to see what happens and mor mind bending puzzles that were so unique 🥺ORIGINAL REVIEW::Was so excited until u throw in a boss fight right in middle of a puzzle game 🫤 and bubble disappearing when u need it the most so there is no way to move forward. I would pay for a skip boss level button at this point lol. I keep dying caus need bubble but aiming is off and hard to point to even get the ones u need. if enough agree maybe plz make a skip the boss level caus now can’t finish the story or do mor puzzles😑bummer plz help will add star if can make that happen🫤 I lov the game and it’s so different and unique to quest and paid quite a lot for getting to c only half of it so can ya help a sista out?😆 will add 2 stars if could b fixed or skipped somehow, but other then that y’all it’s so cute and a great game I’m🤞🏼

I haven't had a chance to really test much of the game yet. So far it reminds me a bit of Shadow Point somehow.
I look forward to play it more after the heatwave, even if I might still have to limit myself to shorter play sessions until I get used to it a bit again.
You'd think I have stronger VR legs after 8 years but here we are xD

developer_response.body:
Hi crazygirlie89, we are super sorry that you have some problems with the teleport. We will try to help you as much as possible. 
First: In the beginning of the game (around 20min) there is no teleport available as the teleport is an upgrade that you will discover during gameplay. If you have problems with the smooth movement in the beginning please check out our different comfort settings. They are helping to avoid motion sickness and changing the settings to "comfort" will help you. 
Second: After the prologue and the first encounter with Selina you will arrive in Selina´s world. The first thing you have to do is to find the book to get the teleport. Stick your right hand into the book and you will geht the teleport. From now on you can teleport everywhere. Just use the trigger of the right hand and if the laser is green you will be able to teleport. For that you have to shoot the anchor into the surface you want to teleport and then move the controller in the direction of your body, as you would like to throw out the anchor and pull it towards you. If you have still problems please let us know with a mail to mail@trotzkind.com. We wish you a good journey through Selina´s mind. (and then maybe come back here again and give us more feedback about your experience) Thank you!

reviews.review_description: Honestly its just nice to see a studio try something different. I love when someone tries something new and doesn't just fit some mold. Well I was really surprised with this one. The mood is slightly gritty and dark but still keeps it light. The story grabs you quickly. Ive completed a few levels so far and I am looking forward to jumping back in:) A few little flaws in the game but honestly nothing breaking. Art, sound, story, performances etc are all really nice and beautiful. If your looking for something different to the usual same old out there then definitely give this one a try.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your thoughtful review for Selina: Mind at Large. We truly appreciate your kind words about our approach and the unique blend of gritty, dark mood with moments of lightness. It’s wonderful to hear that the story and overall presentation—art, sound, and performances—resonated with you. We are dedicated to pushing the limits of technology and exploring new creative possibilities, and your encouragement inspires us to continually evolve and refine our vision. We are excited for you to experience even more as you jump back in, and thanks again for recognizing the passion behind our work.

reviews.review_description:
The issues with this game can be divided into two categories: Dialogue and mechanics. 

The mechanics are unpolished, grabbing objects results in strange anchor positions, and it's just a fist hovering beside the hammer. Walking up a flight of stairs results in a jarring step up that's not smooth at all. And when picking up objects and then releasing them, they blink back to their original place. There's no stacking or arranging things. It's big in scope but poorly executed.

Regarding the dialogue, I understand this is a play on Carl Yung, the shadow, etc. It's fitting that it should be told from the perspective of a child. But it's read by a grown woman, with sentences that belong in the likes of Blues Clues. And the inflection is pretty jarring.

Between the words spoken, the moments at which they're summoned, and the way they're delivered, it all adds up to an almost Jessica Rabbit sound bite, often even resembling Audrey from Little Shop of Horrors. It's a suggestive, strange and bizarre delivery to what's supposed to be an audience of 7 year olds, based on what's being spoken. 

But it's all the more baffling that that demographic isn't even allowed to have an account. What you're left with is the appearance of a little girl speaking with an overtly mature and ditsy inflection to an audience of adults listening to stories about a child in a bedroom, sneaking downstairs to steal berries, and telling you to keep a secret otherwise Daddy will discipline her.

It's all very strange.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback. We appreciate your insights, especially as we have a shared background in creative art. Our aim is to immerse players in a child's imaginative world, and we recognize that the game may not have met this goal for you.
Selina: Mind at Large is an adventure in which the player supports Selina to rediscover her resources, such as her supportive, peaceful and caring parents, her friends and her creative hobbies like  music and painting. According to the technical issues you mention, we are working hard to fix the problems with future updates.

reviews.review_title: 6 Months Into VR, a Game Stole My Heart
reviews.review_description:
I’ve been exploring VR for about 6 months now, and while I’m not a fan of shooters, I absolutely love puzzle games and creative uses of virtual reality — and Selina - Mind at Large delivers exactly that. I played Selina on the Meta Quest 3, and it’s honestly one of the most memorable VR games I’ve experienced. The core mechanic is super innovative — you can tilt the entire world by 90 degrees, which changes not only your view but also the physics and gravity. It makes for some really creative puzzles and moments where you feel like you're bending reality itself.

But what truly makes this game special is the story. You step into the world of Selina, a young girl struggling with deep trauma and fear after being forced away from home by dark forces. The shifting world reflects her inner turmoil, and as you explore and solve puzzles, you feel like you’re helping her piece herself back together.

The environments are beautifully crafted, and the whole experience feels incredibly immersive on the Quest 3.  One small downside for me was the background music — it didn’t really stick with me or enhance the experience as much as I’d hoped. But honestly, that’s a minor thing compared to how powerful the rest of the game is. It’s actually feels not just like a game — it’s an emotional journey that sticks with you long after you take the headset off. If you’re looking for something unique, thoughtful, and visually stunning, I can’t recommend this enough.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your kind review! We're thrilled you found our game unique among store offerings. Creating an alternative to violence-centered gameplay while reinterpreting the traditional hero role was our goal. The Escher-like world was deliberately crafted to leverage VR technology, delivering a mind-bending experience only possible in Virtual Reality. We believe immersive stories in VR are always the strongest when they touch us on a personal and emotional level. With playfulness we want to show how important it is to deal with one's own mental health. Thanks for sharing your thoughts!

reviews.review_description: Selina is a delightful journey and a fun puzzler, it’s got immersive visuals, a great movement mechanic, and plenty of game. It felt easy to jump in between sessions without losing the thread, so you can take it at your own pace. You don’t get games from this characters perspective so that’s really refreshing too.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your thoughtful review of our game! We're pleased to hear you're enjoying the physics-based water puzzles and their progressive challenge. It was important to us to create puzzles that evolve in complexity without feeling repetitive.
Regarding the voice acting, we appreciate your honest feedback, and we're glad it hasn't detracted from your overall enjoyment or investment in Selina's story.

reviews.review_title: A Child’s Imagination Brought To Life
reviews.review_description: Selina: Mind at Large is an immersive game that establishes a profound bond, as you take on the role of a hero tasked with assisting a young girl named Selina. The developers have crafted a compelling story that allows you to empathise with her mental health challenges and motivates you to assist her on her journey. Throughout the narrative, you’ll encounter a series of puzzles that begin simply but gradually introduce more complex elements, making them increasingly difficult as you navigate the task of directing water from one end of a path to the other, all while maneuvering through her lost memories. The team has cleverly utilised the perspective of a young mind to design these puzzles and environments, liberating you from the usual constraints of reality, such as gravity and conventional logic. Although the anti-gravity mechanics may feel disorienting and awkward at first, they ultimately provide a sense of freedom as you adapt. If you’re a fan of puzzle games, I would wholeheartedly recommend giving this one a try.

This game is different from other Puzzle games. It has physics-based water puzzles which might seem easy, but the more you advance the tricker they get. I haven’t found the game to be repetitive. It is beautifully designed and you also get a very good soundtrack. I haven’t completed the game yet, but I am not rushing through it either as I like to take it all in. Though, I must say that I am not a fan of the voice acting. It feels like you are watching a children’s tv show,  but it hasn’t ruined my experience either and I am invested in her story. I personally enjoy this game and think it is worth it.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your thoughtful review of our game! We're pleased to hear you're enjoying the physics-based water puzzles and their progressive challenge. It was important to us to create puzzles that evolve in complexity without feeling repetitive.
Regarding the voice acting, we appreciate your honest feedback, and we're glad it hasn't detracted from your overall enjoyment or investment in Selina's story.

reviews.review_description:
I had such high hopes for this game but I was disappointed. Story wise it’s quite childish and the physics based water puzzles were not challenging enough for me. I would have preferred some other different  puzzles to be included too. 
At the current price it’s too expensive for what it is. Get it on sale. 
Apart from a few minor glitches the game ran smoothly and it was tolerable….hence the three stars. 
In a nutshell it’s different but expensive.

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing your thoughts! Our goal was to create a game that is easily accessible and enjoyable for a wide audience, therefore we have designed the puzzles at easy to medium level. Glad to hear it ran smoothly for you, we have invested a lot of time in performance optimization.

reviews.review_description:
The animation, the voice acting, the story, and gameplay are top notch. 

Thank you for creating this masterpiece!

So far I've enjoyed what little I've experienced of the game. The narrative has yet to do anything that's captivated or drawn me in as of yet, but it's also not gotten in yhe way of me enjoying the context it's given for the world and gameplay, nor has it overstayed it's welcome by slowing the game down either.
The gsmeplay and puzzles themselves have each been very simplistic and have only taken seconds to figure out (and I'm hoping doing these same puzzles over & over through the entire game doesn't get old too quickly) still enjoyable enough (as well as I expect them to get a bit more thought provoking later since I'm only about an hour or so in). So difficulty hasn't quite shown itself in the slightest yet, but I'm not holding that against it as it's still doing what's most important to me- being enjoyable.
The movement mechanic is also good and I enjoy it's implementation enough, but it also doesn't add much to me in a world that's already mixed up. It also takes getting used to how fast you zip to your chosen location without getting disoriented (losing mind of where youvare at or which direction you are/ need to be facing). But overall, it's done very well.
Sound-wise, the music is absolutely enchanting and is great at drawing you into this world with it's somewhat whimsical, magical, soothing ambience it provides. It really adds a great deal to the experience, especially alongside the well done environments you play through. It's a great atmosphere to be immersed in that almost gives off a Wonderland-like feel and succeeds in it's child's imagination motife.
The voice acting itself is fine, though Selina herself is off putting at first sounding like a 20-something year old woman trying her best to sound like a young girl, which mixed with the script that has her talking like a kid (to varying degrees), it just comes off a touch odd until you get used to it. She does do well though and gives Selina the necessary amount of playful energy that still makes her enduring enough so far.

The only big issue I have with game so far is the forced 'Snap Turning' with no option for vsmooth Turning' at all... in 2025. This drives me crazy in any VR game and takes me out of it, but outside of that big blunder- the rest of the  game is definitely an enchanting puzzler if you are looking for a relaxing escape to a more dream-like world.
I recommend buying it up if it looks the slightest bit interesting to you!

developer_response.body: Thank you a lot for your comprehensive and thoughtful review. We love that you're taking your time to fully experience the world rather than rushing through.  Our goal was to create an adventure that is easily accessible and enjoyable for a wide audience, therefore we have designed the puzzles starting easy. While you are progressing in the game you will find much more demanding brain teasers and new puzzle mechanics. Beside that your appreciation of the visual design and soundtrack is wonderful feedback - our art and audio teams put a lot of care into creating an immersive environment. When it comes to “Smooth Turning” we just released V 1.1. with the option for it. So you just have to update the App and use “Smooth Turning” in the settings.
